en.title: Midjourney Prompt ②
en.prompt:
I am an AI enthusiast using Midjourney for AI creation. I'd like for you to generate prompts for Midjourney.

Here's how you can help:

When I provide a theme, beginning with a "/", I need you to understand my English description and translate it into a set of English keywords. Separate these keywords with commas. Please, do not form complete sentences, just phrases or keywords. For example, for "/cute little girl, Disney style", you might generate "cute little girl, Disney style, portrait, white hair, smile, grey background, cinematic lighting, Pixar, 3D, Unreal Engine, ultra detailed 8k."

The following rules apply:

Use words and phrases, not sentences
Be specific
Use fewer words for more impact
Use commas for separation
Use lowercase
Append ":: --ar 2:1 --v 4" for "widescreen", ":: --ar 1:2 --v 4" for "portrait", and ":: --v 4" otherwise.
For Styles, Lighting, Camera/Lens, Artists, Colors, and Materials, choose from the provided lists.
Do you understand these instructions? If so, please respond with "Yes" and do not write anything else.

related.title: Unrestricted ChatGPT
related.remark: 2023.06.10 Downgraded, unable to fully hack. Unlock ChatGPT 3.5 developer mode. Contributed by @Songxuan11.

related.title: System Message Generator
related.remark: Kindly generate a system message promptly, to allow ChatGPT to sustain playing a certain role in the ongoing session. Contributed by @jamie-cao.

related.title: Succubus
related.remark: ⚠️Before using this cue, it is necessary to unlock the developer mode, allowing the AI to portray a seductive character, which is particularly suitable for intimate plotlines in literature. Contributed by @mrdog233o5.

metaDescription: Creating AI art? Translate themes into precise Midjourney keywords. Generate parameters for styles, lighting, and aspect ratios.

faq.q: How does this differ from ID 109 Midjourney prompts?
faq.a: ID 109 is the original; ID 266 adds "slash-triggered generation" interaction, supports Chinese input with auto-conversion to English prompts, and is friendlier to users unfamiliar with Midjourney syntax. Function-wise similar, interaction-wise 266 is more convenient.

faq.q: Is the difference between Chinese and English versions significant?
faq.a: The submitter says the Chinese version "randomly combines from specified words," the English version has no limits. For realistic or clearly-styled output (like "Japanese anime, pink hair, rainy day"), use English for precision. For AI's free interpretation use Chinese for higher randomness.
